stem: A kite is flying at the end of a 60-foot string that makes a 53-degree angle with the ground. Given that sin 53° ≈ 4/5, cos 53° ≈ 3/5, and tan 53° ≈ 4/3, what is the height of the kite above the ground, in feet?
explanation: The kite string (60 feet) is the hypotenuse of the right triangle, and the height of the kite is the side opposite the 53° angle. Using sine: sin 53° = opposite/hypotenuse = height/60. Therefore, height = 60 × sin 53° = 60 × (4/5) = 48 feet.

distractorRationale.A: Used cos instead of sin, finding the horizontal distance instead of the height: 60 × cos 53° = 60 × (3/5) = 36.
distractorRationale.B: Set up the sine ratio inverted, dividing by sin instead of multiplying: height = 60 ÷ (4/5) = 60 × (5/4) = 75.
distractorRationale.C: Used tan instead of sin, treating the hypotenuse as the adjacent side: 60 × tan 53° = 60 × (4/3) = 80.

imageDescription: A right triangle with the hypotenuse labeled as a 60 ft kite string extending from a point on the ground up to a kite. The angle between the string and the ground is labeled 53°. The vertical leg is labeled with a question mark representing the height of the kite. A small kite icon is at the top of the vertical leg.
